seoTitle: Add Supplier Purchase Invoices in QuickBooks
metaDescription: Record incoming vendor bills in QuickBooks Online, from manual entry to OCR receipt scanning, to keep your accounts payable accurate.
intro: Keeping track of what you owe suppliers starts with recording their invoices as bills. This guide covers how to enter a supplier purchase invoice in QuickBooks Online manually, and how to speed up the process using automated receipt scanning.

steps.heading: Open the Bill form
steps.body: Click + New and select Bill from the Vendors column.

steps.heading: Select or add the vendor
steps.body: Choose an existing supplier or quickly create a new vendor profile.

steps.heading: Enter invoice details
steps.body: Type the supplier's invoice number in the Bill No. field and set net payment terms to auto-calculate the due date.

steps.heading: Categorize the purchase
steps.body: In the Category Details grid, select the correct expense line, enter the amount, and add a memo note.

steps.heading: Or use OCR upload
steps.body: Alternatively, go to Expenses, then Receipts, and drag your supplier invoice PDF into the upload zone to auto-extract key fields.

faq.question: What's the difference between a Bill and an Expense in QuickBooks?
faq.answer: A Bill tracks an unpaid supplier invoice as accounts payable, while an Expense records a payment that's already been made.

faq.question: Can QuickBooks auto-fill vendor bill details?
faq.answer: Yes, uploading a PDF invoice through the Receipts tool uses OCR to auto-populate vendor and amount fields.

faq.question: How do I track what I still owe suppliers?
faq.answer: Run the Accounts Payable Aging Summary report to monitor outstanding bills by vendor and due date.

faq.question: Can I set custom payment terms per vendor?
faq.answer: Yes, you can select net terms like Net 30 or Net 15 on each bill to control the calculated due date.
